steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks how Text 2 responds to the problem described in Text 1. Text 1 establishes that differing national definitions make it impossible to compare urbanization rates across countries. To find Text 2's function, look for what it adds in relation to that problem.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘By recommending that a specific institution should further investigate the problem’: Text 2 doesn't call for more research — it describes something already developed. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘By suggesting that researchers focus on topics besides urbanization’: Nothing in Text 2 suggests giving up. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘By noting that a possible solution to the problem is available’: Text 2 describes a new measure with global criteria that international organizations recently developed — a direct solution to Text 1's comparability problem. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘By supplying additional ways in which urbanization research is difficult’: Text 2 doesn't raise new complications; it offers a remedy. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
